methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $1.1B last post money.

data.narrative:
Skyryse, a Series C-stage space and aerospace company, carries a current post-money valuation of $1.15B following $500M+ in total capital raised. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 5 public tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers, with particular weight placed on 4 same-stage, same-sector private comparables: Axiom Space ($2.5B), Impulse Space ($1.8B), Varda Space Industries ($1.5B), and Isar Aerospace ($1.0B).

Two triangulation methods prove applicable. The stage-progression approach, applying a 1.7x step-up multiplier to the $1.15B last post-money, implies a forward valuation of $2.0B. The peer-median method across the four same-sector, Series C comparables yields a median valuation of $1.6B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $2.5B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied in the absence of disclosed revenue figures.

These methods triangulate to a valuation range of $1.4B to $2.2B, with a median implied value of $1.8B. The current $1.15B valuation sits materially below both implied midpoints, reflecting either market pricing at a discount to peer medians or potential variations in operational metrics and risk profile not captured in stage and sector taxonomy. Key uncertainties include revenue trajectory, margin profile, and competitive positioning within the aerospace subsector.
